content.content:
{"result":"Found 10 search results:

1. GDB to LLDB command map - LLVM
   URL: https://lldb.llvm.org/use/map.html
   Summary: GDB toLLDBcommandmap # Below is a table of GDBcommandswith theirLLDBcounterparts. The built in GDB-compatibility aliases inLLDBare also listed. The fulllldbcommandnames are often long, but any unique short form can be used. Instead of " breakpoint set ", " br se " is also acceptable. ExecutionCommandsBreakpointCommandsWatchpointCommandsExamining Variables Evaluating ...

2. Debug your app | Android Studio | Android Developers
   URL: https://developer.android.com/studio/debug/
   Summary: From the UseAndroidDebugger Settings from menu, you can select an existing run/debug configuration. For C and C++ code, this lets you reuse theLLDBstartupcommands,LLDBpost-attachcommands, and symbol directories in an existing configuration. If you don't have an existing run/debug configuration, select Create New.

3. The LLDB Debugger - Part 3: Watchpoint - kyryl horbushko
   URL: https://khorbushko.github.io/article/2021/05/27/the-lldb-debugger-part3-watchpoint.html
   Summary: watch the memory context adding thewatchpointoptions set type set conditions edit list delete understanding context Resources Debugging - is not just aprocessfor finding and fixing bugs - for me, this is a great way to find out how actually the program works. To do so, we should be able to detect any change at any memory address. Often, breakpoints can help a lot within this task, but for ...

4. How do I use lldb to debug C++ code on Android on command line
   URL: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/53733781/how-do-i-use-lldb-to-debug-c-code-on-android-on-command-line
   Summary: How can I debug myAndroidNDK project in C++, using thelldbdebugger from thecommandline?

5. source/Commands/CommandObjectWatchpointCommand.cpp - platform/external ...
   URL: https://android.googlesource.com/platform/external/lldb/+/refs/heads/main/source/Commands/CommandObjectWatchpointCommand.cpp
   Summary: \n\ (lldb)watchpointcommandadd -s python 1 \n\ Enter your Python command(s).Type'DONE' to end. \n\ > def watchpoint_output (wp_no): \n\ > out_string = \"Hitwatchpointnumber \" + repr (wp_no) \n\ > print out_string \n\ >returnTrue \n\ > watchpoint_output (1) \n\ > DONE \n\ \n\ \n\ Example multiple line Pythonwatchpointcommand,using'loose ...

6. LLDB Cheatsheet - A Quick Reference For Useful Debugging Commands
   URL: https://firexfly.com/lldb-cheatsheet/
   Summary: The most useful debuggingcommandsare right on your fingertips but the exact syntax has slipped your mind. TheLLDBcheatsheet will refresh your memory.

7. Android LLDB debugging - IREE
   URL: https://iree.dev/developers/debugging/android-with-lldb/
   Summary: AndroidLLDBdebugging This doc shows how to useLLDBto debug native binaries onAndroid. For a more complete explanation, see the officialLLDBdocumentation on remote debugging. Prerequisites We assume the following setup:AndroidNDK is installed and the ANDROID_NDK environment variable is set to the installation path.

8. LLDB Watchpoint Commands - rohinibarla.com
   URL: https://rohinibarla.com/posts/lldb-watchpoint-commands/
   Summary: Thoughts on software, programming, education and life.LLDBWatchpointCommandsWritten on December 08, 2025 1 min. read

9. LLDB Debugger commands · GitHub
   URL: https://gist.github.com/wanyakun/314690093ea195d749fca6869ebf200e
   Summary: Debuggercommands: apropos -- List debuggercommandsrelated to a word or subject. breakpoint --Commandsfor operating on breakpoints (see 'help b' for shorthand.) bugreport --Commandsfor creating domain-specific bug reports.command--Commandsfor managing customLLDBcommands. disassemble -- Disassemble specified instructions in the current target. Defaults to the current function for ...

10. LLDB Commands - Dash Cheat Sheets - Kapeli
   URL: https://kapeli.com/cheat_sheets/LLDB_Commands.docset/Contents/Resources/Documents/index
   Summary: LLDBCommandscheat sheet for Dash - LLVM debuggercommandsfor debugging C, C++, Objective-C, and Swift applications. Essential debugging tool for Xcode and iOS development. Download Dash for macOS to access this and other cheat sheets offline.
"}

content.content: {"query":"LLDB Android debugging attach process watch commands","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://iree.dev/developers/debugging/android-with-lldb/","title":"Android LLDB debugging - IREE","content":"This doc shows how to use LLDB to debug native binaries on Android. For a more complete explanation, see the official LLDB documentation on remote debugging. 1. Android NDK is installed and the `ANDROID_NDK` environment variable is set to the installation path. 2. Your Android device connected and configured for `adb`. 3. The Android binary of interest is already compiled and the command to run it (in `adb shell`) is  `[program args...]`. 1. Push the toolchain files, including `lldb-server`, to your device:. You may need to adjust the clang toolchain version to match the one in your NDK. You can find it with `find \"$ANDROID_NDK/toolchains/llvm/prebuilt\" -name lldb-server`. We are going to use port 5039 but you are free to pick a different one:. 4. Launch `lldb`, connect to the server and run the binary:. Then, we tell lldb which working directory to use, where to find the executable, and what command line arguments to use.","score":0.6244086,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://issuetracker.google.com/issues/145707569","title":"LLDB Startup Commands Can't work with `Attach Debugger to ...","content":"Follow the steps: Android Studio 3.5.2 -> Run/Debug Configuration -> Switch to Debugger Tab -> LLDB Startup Commands -> add my commands.","score":0.61363083,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://intellij-support.jetbrains.com/hc/en-us/community/posts/33561881361682-Where-is-RustRover-Topic-I-cannot-remotely-debug-android-app-with-RustRover-LLDB-Fails-to-Attach-No-Stop-Reply-Packet-Received","title":"Where is RustRover Topic? I cannot remotely debug android app with RustRover LLDB Fails to Attach - No Stop Reply Packet Received","content":"I cannot remotely debug android app with RustRover LLDB Fails to Attach - No Stop Reply Packet Received – IDEs Support (IntelliJ Platform) | JetBrains. Our website uses some cookies and records your IP address for the purposes of accessibility, security, and managing your access to the telecommunication network. You can disable data collection and cookies by changing your browser settings, but it may affect how this website functions. With your consent, JetBrains may also use cookies and your IP address to collect individual statistics and provide you with personalized offers and ads subject to the Privacy Notice and the Terms of Use. JetBrains may use third-party services for this purpose. You can adjust or withdraw your consent at any time by visiting the Opt-Out. IDEs Support (IntelliJ Platform) | JetBrains. 1.   IDEs Support (IntelliJ Platform) | JetBrains. I cannot remotely debug android app with RustRover LLDB Fails to Attach - No Stop Reply Packet Received. adb shell \"run-as ${APP} cp /data/local/tmp/lldb-server /data/data/${APP}/lldb-server\". adb shell \"run-as ${APP} /data/data/${APP}/lldb-server platform --listen *:5039 --server\".","score":0.60498303,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://stackoverflow.com/questions/53733781/how-do-i-use-lldb-to-debug-c-code-on-android-on-command-line","title":"How do I use lldb to debug C++ code on Android on command line","content":"1. Preparation 2. Compile program with debugging info (ie keep the -g flag) After compilation, copy them to your device's /data/local/tmp directory.","score":0.40839532,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.kodeco.com/books/advanced-apple-debugging-reverse-engineering/v3.0/chapters/3-attaching-with-lldb","title":"Advanced Apple Debugging & Reverse Engineering, Chapter 3: Attaching with LLDB | Kodeco","content":"Third Edition · iOS 12 · Swift 4.2 · Xcode 10. #### Section I: Beginning LLDB Commands. + 1.1 Getting around Rootless. + 1.2 Attaching LLDB to Xcode. + 2.1 The “help” command. + 2.2 The “apropos” command. + 4.2 LLDB breakpoint syntax. + 4.3 Finally… creating breakpoints. + 5.1 Formatting p and po. + 5.2 Swift vs Objective-C debugging contexts. + 5.3 User defined variables. + 6.2 Examining the stack’s frames. + 6.4 Examining data in the stack. + 8.2 Finding a property’s offset. + 8.3 What caused the watchpoint. + 8.4 The Xcode GUI watchpoint equivalent. + 9.2 Creating the .lldbinit file. + 9.3 Command aliases with arguments. + 11.3 Objective-C and registers. + 11.5 Swift and registers. + 12.2 The RIP register. + 12.3 Registers and breaking up the bits. + 12.4 Breaking down the memory. + 13.1 The stack, revisited. + 13.5 The stack and 7+ parameters. + 13.6 The stack and debugging info. + 19.2 The Mach-O sections.","score":0.3525366,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.4,"request_id":"66a92835-835f-439a-a234-6ecf6534ca25"}

results.title: Android LLDB debugging - IREE
results.content: This doc shows how to use LLDB to debug native binaries on Android. For a more complete explanation, see the official LLDB documentation on remote debugging. 1. Android NDK is installed and the `ANDROID_NDK` environment variable is set to the installation path. 2. Your Android device connected and configured for `adb`. 3. The Android binary of interest is already compiled and the command to run it (in `adb shell`) is  `[program args...]`. 1. Push the toolchain files, including `lldb-server`, to your device:. You may need to adjust the clang toolchain version to match the one in your NDK. You can find it with `find "$ANDROID_NDK/toolchains/llvm/prebuilt" -name lldb-server`. We are going to use port 5039 but you are free to pick a different one:. 4. Launch `lldb`, connect to the server and run the binary:. Then, we tell lldb which working directory to use, where to find the executable, and what command line arguments to use.

content.thinking:
The user is asking about how to use LLDB to attach to an Android app process, how to watch (watchpoints), and how to execute commands. Based on the search results, I have enough information to provide a comprehensive answer about LLDB debugging on Android.

Let me compile the information:

1. **Attach to Android app process**:
   - Need to set up lldb-server on the Android device
   - Use `adb shell` to push lldb-server to the device
   - Start lldb-server in platform mode listening on a port (e.g., 5039)
   - From local machine, connect via `lldb` and then `process connect ...`

2. **Watch (watchpoints)**:
   - `watchpoint set variable <name>` - watch a variable
   - `watchpoint set expression -- <addr>` - watch a memory address
   - `watchpoint list` - list watchpoints
   - `watchpoint delete <id>` - delete watchpoint
   - Various watchpoint commands available

3. **Execute commands**:
   - Basic commands like `breakpoint set`, `continue`, `next`, `step`, `print`, etc.

Let me provide a structured answer in Chinese as the user asked in Chinese.

content.text:
# LLDB 调试 Android 应用指南

## 一、Attach 进程

### 方式 1：通过 lldb-server（推荐）

**Step 1: 推送 lldb-server 到设备**
```bash
# 找到 lldb-server（NDK 内）
find "$ANDROID_NDK/toolchains/llvm/prebuilt" -name lldb-server

# 推送到设备
adb push lldb-server /data/local/tmp/
adb shell chmod 755 /data/local/tmp/lldb-server
```

**Step 2: 在设备上启动 lldb-server**
```bash
# 使用 run-as 避免权限问题（APP 为包名）
adb shell "run-as com.example.myapp cp /data/local/tmp/lldb-server /data/data/com.example.myapp/lldb-server"
adb shell "run-as com.example.myapp /data/data/com.example.myapp/lldb-server platform --listen *:5039 --server"
```

**Step 3: 本地连接**
```bash
# 启动 lldb
lldb

# 连接远程 server
(lldb) process connect connect://<设备IP>:5039
```

### 方式 2：使用 ADB 端口转发

```bash
# 端口转发
adb forward tcp:5039 tcp:5039

# 连接
(lldb) process connect connect://127.0.0.1:5039
```

---

## 二、Watch（监视点 / Watchpoint）

```bash
# 监视变量（自动设置地址）
(lldb) watchpoint set variable myVar

# 监视内存地址
(lldb) watchpoint set expression -- 0x7fff1234

# 列出所有 watchpoint
(lldb) watchpoint list

# 设置条件
(lldb) watchpoint set variable globalVar -c "(globalVar > 10)"

# 删除 watchpoint
(lldb) watchpoint delete <id>

# 监视读写操作（默认是读写都监视）
(lldb) watchpoint set variable ptr --wrtype read   # 只监视读
(lldb) watchpoint set variable ptr --wrtype write  # 只监视写
```

---

## 三、执行控制命令

```bash
# 断点
(lldb) breakpoint set --name functionName
(lldb) br s -n main  # 简写形式

# 继续运行
(lldb) continue
(lldb) c

# 单步执行（源码行）
(lldb) next
(lldb) n

# 单步执行（指令）
(lldb) step
(lldb) s

# 单步跳出
(lldb) finish

# 运行到某行
(lldb) thread step-over  # 执行到下一行

# 打印变量
(lldb) print myVar
(lldb) po myVar  # Objective-C 对象描述

# 打印寄存器
(lldb) register read

# 打印内存
(lldb) memory read 0x7fff1234
(lldb) x 0x7fff1234  # 简写

# 修改变量值
(lldb) expr myVar = 42
```

---

## 四、完整示例流程

```bash
# 1. 设备端启动 lldb-server
adb shell "run-as com.example.myapp /data/data/com.example.myapp/lldb-server platform --listen *:5039 --server" &

# 2. 本地端口转发
adb forward tcp:5039 tcp:5039

# 3. lldb 连接
lldb
(lldb) process connect connect://127.0.0.1:5039

# 4. 设置符号路径（可选）
(lldb) target create --symfile ./app.so

# 5. 设置断点
(lldb) breakpoint set --name Java_com_example_myapp_MainActivity_onCreate

# 6. 继续运行
(lldb) c

# 7. 监视变量
(lldb) watchpoint set variable myCounter

# 8. 继续触发 watchpoint
(lldb) c
```

---

## 五、常用 LLDB 命令速查

| 功能 | 命令 |
|------|------|
| 帮助 | `help` |
| 列出进程 | `process list` |
| Attach | `process attach --pid <PID>` |
| 断点 | `breakpoint set -n <函数名>` |
| 单步 | `next` / `step` / `finish` |
| 打印 | `print <expr>` / `po <obj>` |
| Watch | `watchpoint set variable <name>` |
| 线程 | `thread list` / `thread select <id>` |
| 栈帧 | `backtrace` / `frame select <id>` |

---

**知识来源**: Web搜索（四引擎并行）- "LLDB Android attach process watchpoint commands"
